modules.title: Module 1 — Groundstrokes + Serve Intro (Beginner)
modules.description: Introduction to forehand and backhand groundstrokes using cones and reduced court. First serve session focusing on grip, motion, and getting the ball in.
modules.content:
MODULE 1 — GROUNDSTROKES + SERVE INTRO
Level: Beginner | Date: Tuesday 3 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Free rally in a reduced court using yellow marks on the middle. Players work on keeping the ball in play, slow and controlled.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — FOREHAND
Drill: 1 player in the back hitting forehands. Use a cone behind the player to make them recover position after each shot. Use a cone on the opposite side to guide their target direction.
Rally: 2 players in the back, forehand to forehand. Goal: keep the ball in play with long rallies. Court reduced with yellow marks.

EXERCISE 2 — BACKHAND
Drill: Same structure but for backhand.
Rally: 2 players in the back, backhand to backhand. Same reduced court setup.

SECOND PART — SERVE INTRO + GAME (25 min)

Serve Practice: 1-2 players serving from each side. Coach demonstrates grip and motion. Focus on getting the ball in, not power. Rotate after 5 serves each.

Game 1: 4 players all in the back. Coach feeds the ball, rally starts. Goal: reach 10 consecutive shots without missing. Communicate!

Game 2: Same setup. Each team has 5 lives. Lose a life when you miss. First team to 0 lives loses.

If time left: Tiebreak to 10, all players starting from the back.

modules.title: Module 1 — Groundstrokes + Serve Intro (Intermediate)
modules.description: Forehand and backhand groundstroke drills with deeper ball targets on a full court. Serve practice focused on consistent motion and placement, followed by net game rallies.
modules.content:
MODULE 1 — GROUNDSTROKES + SERVE INTRO
Level: Intermediate | Date: Tuesday 3 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Free rally, full court. Mix of groundstrokes, moving side to side.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — FOREHAND
Drill: 1 player in the back hitting forehands. Cone to recover position, cone to guide target.
Rally: 2 players in the back, forehand to forehand. Ball must bounce before the service line (deep and controlled).

EXERCISE 2 — BACKHAND
Drill: Same structure but for backhand.
Rally: 2 players in the back, backhand to backhand. Ball must bounce before the service line.

SECOND PART — SERVE + GAME (25 min)

Serve Practice: Players serve and the other returns. Rotate after 5 serves. Focus on consistent motion and placement.

Game 1: 2 players in the back, 2 at the net. Coach feeds ball. Goal: 10-15 consecutive shots.

Game 2: Same formation. Each team has 5 lives. Lose a life on a miss.

If time left: Tiebreak to 10, starting from the back.

modules.title: Module 1 — Groundstrokes + Serve Intro (Advanced)
modules.description: Forehand and backhand combined with side glass recovery. Tactical points introducing glass defense and net dominance, including King of the Court.
modules.content:
MODULE 1 — GROUNDSTROKES + SERVE INTRO
Level: Advanced | Date: Tuesday 3 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court warm up including overhead smashes.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — FOREHAND + SIDE GLASS
Drill: 1 player in the back. Forehand plus immediate side glass recovery. Cone to mark recovery position.
Rally: 2 players in the back. One plays down the line, the other defends using forehand off the glass.

EXERCISE 2 — BACKHAND + SIDE GLASS
Drill: Same structure but backhand, using the glass.
Rally: 1 in the back, 1 at the net. Net player directs ball to the backhand. Back player aims down the line or cross.

SECOND PART — TACTICAL POINTS (25 min)

Points 1: 2 in the back, 2 at the net. Normal coach-fed points. Net team controls the point — no passive volleys.

Points 2: Back players must use the glass at least once per point. If they win the rally off the glass, it counts double.

Last 10 min: King of the court.

modules.title: Module 2 — Volleys + Net Game + Serve (Beginner)
modules.description: Introduction to forehand and backhand volleys at the net with cone guidance. Serve practice focusing on stance and toss, followed by net-vs-back games.
modules.content:
MODULE 2 — VOLLEYS + NET GAME + SERVE
Level: Beginner | Date: Friday 8 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Reduced court rally. Double-touch warm up: players must tap the ball once with the racket before hitting it over.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — FOREHAND VOLLEY
Drill: 1 player at the net hitting forehand volleys. Cone behind to recover to the T position. Cone on the other side to guide where to aim.
Rally: 1 at the net, 1 in the back. Volleys must land before service line.

EXERCISE 2 — BACKHAND VOLLEY
Drill: Same structure but backhand volley.
Rally: 1 at the net, 1 in the back. Volleys only from the net player.

SECOND PART — SERVE + GAME (25 min)

Serve Practice: Focus on stance and toss. Rotate after 5 serves each.

Game 1: 2 at the net, 2 in the back. No lobs, only flat shots. Goal: 10-15 consecutive shots.

Game 2: 4 players start in the back. Coach feeds to one side — those two go to the net. Other side plays flat only. Tiebreak to 10.

modules.title: Module 2 — Volleys + Net Game + Serve (Intermediate)
modules.description: Forehand and backhand volley drills progressing to volley-to-volley exchanges. Serve and return practice with glass bonuses and net approach games.
modules.content:
MODULE 2 — VOLLEYS + NET GAME + SERVE
Level: Intermediate | Date: Friday 8 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court rally including going to the net (no overhead smash yet).

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — FOREHAND VOLLEY
Drill: 1 player at the net, forehand volley.
Rally 1: 1 at the net, 1 in the back. Net player volleys only (before service line).
Rally 2 (progression): Volley-to-volley between the two net players.

EXERCISE 2 — BACKHAND VOLLEY
Drill: Same structure but backhand.
Rally 1: Groundstrokes vs volleys.
Rally 2: Volley-to-volley after the drill.

SECOND PART — SERVE + GAME (25 min)

Serve Practice: One serves, one returns. Returner picks a side and commits. Rotate every 5.

Game 1: 2 at the net, 2 in the back. No lobs. Off-glass counts double.

Game 2: 4 players in the back. Coach feeds one side — they go to the net. Tiebreak to 10.

modules.title: Module 2 — Volleys + Net Game + Serve (Advanced)
modules.description: Forehand and backhand volleys combined with drop shot introduction. Tactical points with drop shot requirements and King of the Court.
modules.content:
MODULE 2 — VOLLEYS + NET GAME + SERVE
Level: Advanced | Date: Friday 8 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court warm up with overhead smashes included.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — FOREHAND VOLLEY + DROP SHOT INTRO
Drill: 1 at the net. Coach feeds short balls. Player decides: volley away or drop shot.
Rally: 1 at the net, 1 in the back. Net player mixes volleys and drop shots.

EXERCISE 2 — BACKHAND VOLLEY + DROP SHOT
Drill: Same as above but backhand side.
Rally: Net player uses both backhand volley and drop shot variation.

SECOND PART — TACTICAL POINTS (25 min)

Points 1: Net team must use at least one drop shot per point. Drop shot winner counts double.

Points 2: Tiebreak to 7. Net team cannot smash — only volleys and drop shots.

Last 10 min: King of the court.

modules.title: Module 3 — Back Glass + Transition (Beginner)
modules.description: Learning to defend off the back glass with both forehand and backhand. Transition drills from the glass to the net position.
modules.content:
MODULE 3 — BACK GLASS + TRANSITION
Level: Beginner | Date: Tuesday 10 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Rally in the middle, reduced court.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — FOREHAND OFF THE BACK GLASS
Drill: Coach feeds a deep ball; player hits it after the back glass using a forehand. Cone to mark recovery position, cone for target direction.
Rally: 2 in the back. Both aim deep and down the line, defending using forehand off the glass.

EXERCISE 2 — BACKHAND OFF THE BACK GLASS
Drill: Same structure but backhand.
Rally: 2 in the back. Both aim deep and down the line using backhand off the glass.

SECOND PART — TRANSITION + GAME (25 min)

Tactical 1: 4 in the back. Coach feeds deep. Players return off the glass and move up to the net. Point continues.

Tactical 2: Same setup. Glass win counts double.

If time left: King of the court.

modules.title: Module 3 — Back Glass + Transition (Intermediate)
modules.description: Forehand and backhand off the back glass with progression to passing the net player. Tactical games using glass defense and lob-to-net transitions.
modules.content:
MODULE 3 — BACK GLASS + TRANSITION
Level: Intermediate | Date: Tuesday 10 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court rally, going to the net included (no overhead).

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — FOREHAND OFF THE BACK GLASS
Drill: Forehand off the glass, cone recovery and target.
Rally 1: 2 in the back. Aim down the line, deep.
Rally 2 (progression): 1 in the back, 1 at the net. Back player defends off the glass and tries to pass the net player.

EXERCISE 2 — BACKHAND OFF THE BACK GLASS
Drill: Same structure, backhand side.
Rally 1: 2 in the back. Down the line, deep, backhand off glass.
Rally 2: 1 in the back, 1 at the net. Back player defends backhand off glass.

SECOND PART — TRANSITION + GAME (25 min)

Tactical 1: 2 in the back, 2 at the net. Back players defend off glass, then transition to the net.

Tactical 2: Back players start with a lob, follow it to the net. Net players let the lob bounce — defensive transition.

If time left: King of the court.

modules.title: Module 3 — Back Glass + Transition (Advanced)
modules.description: Mixed side and back glass reading drill, plus rulo introduction at the net. Tactical points with glass and rulo scoring rules.
modules.content:
MODULE 3 — BACK GLASS + TRANSITION
Level: Advanced | Date: Tuesday 10 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court warm up with overheads.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RULO INTRO (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — SIDE GLASS + BACK GLASS (MIXED)
Drill: Coach feeds varied balls to side glass and back glass. Player reads and adapts.
Rally: 2 in the back. Random mix of glass shots.

EXERCISE 2 — RULO INTRO
Drill: 1 at the net. Coach feeds a low ball. Player practises the rulo (wrist-heavy topspin smash, angled towards the side glass). Focus on grip, swing path, follow-through.
Rally: 1 in the back plays a lob; 1 at the net attempts the rulo. Back player practises defensive position after the rulo.

SECOND PART — TACTICAL POINTS (25 min)

Points 1: Back team must use the glass at least once per point. Net team practises rulo when a lob presents.

Points 2: Tiebreak to 7. Rulo winner counts double.

Last 10 min: King of the court.

modules.title: Module 4 — Lob + Bandeja/Vibora (Beginner)
modules.description: Introduction to the offensive lob and first contact with the bandeja and vibora overhead shots. Serve practice with toss and contact point focus.
modules.content:
MODULE 4 — LOB + BANDEJA/VIBORA
Level: Beginner | Date: Friday 15 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Reduced court rally. Focus on height and depth of shots.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — LOB
Drill: 1 at the net hitting lobs. Cone to recover. Cone or zone to aim for (deep, behind service line).
Rally: 2 in the back. Lob to lob — high and deep.

EXERCISE 2 — BANDEJA/VIBORA (INTRO)
Drill: Coach feeds a lob. Player practises bandeja or vibora. Cone for recovery and target.
Rally: 1 in the back, 1 at the net. Back player hits low groundstrokes; net player volleys only, slow and in control.

SECOND PART — SERVE + GAME (25 min)

Serve Practice: 5 serves each. Focus on toss and contact point.

Tactical 1: Coach feeds lob to net players — they start with a bandeja or vibora.

Tactical 2: Coach feeds to back players — they start with a lob. Net players respond with bandeja or vibora.

If time left: Tiebreak to 10.

modules.title: Module 4 — Lob + Bandeja/Vibora (Intermediate)
modules.description: Structured lob and bandeja/vibora rhythm drills with net approach included. Serve and return practice with three tactical game scenarios.
modules.content:
MODULE 4 — LOB + BANDEJA/VIBORA
Level: Intermediate | Date: Friday 15 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court rally. Include going to the net with overhead.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — LOB
Drill: 1 at the net hitting lobs. Cone for recovery, cone or zone for target.
Rally 1: 2 in the back. Lob to lob — high and deep.
Rally 2: 1 in the back, 1 at the net. Low groundstrokes vs volleys (before service line).

EXERCISE 2 — BANDEJA/VIBORA
Drill: Coach feeds lob — player executes bandeja or vibora.
Rally: Back player: one lob, two low shots. Net player: one vibora, two volleys. Structured rhythm.

SECOND PART — SERVE + GAME (25 min)

Serve and Return Practice: One serves, one returns to a target side. Rotate every 5.

Tactical 1: Coach feeds lob to net team — point starts with bandeja or vibora.

Tactical 2: Back team starts with a lob — net team responds.

Tactical 3: Back player varies freely (lobs, low shots, glass). Net team must read and react.

modules.title: Module 4 — Lob + Bandeja/Vibora (Advanced)
modules.description: Full overhead weapon toolkit: bandeja, vibora, rulo, and drop shot. Tactical points where the net team must earn and defend their position.
modules.content:
MODULE 4 — LOB + BANDEJA/VIBORA
Level: Advanced | Date: Friday 15 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court with overheads.

FIRST PART — RULO + DROP SHOT + BANDEJA/VIBORA (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — BANDEJA/VIBORA VS LOB
Drill: Coach alternates lobs to different positions. Player plays bandeja or vibora depending on position and height.
Rally: Back: one lob, two low. Net: one vibora, two volleys.

EXERCISE 2 — RULO + DROP SHOT
Drill: High ball = rulo. Low ball = drop shot. Player reads and decides quickly.
Rally: Net player uses all weapons — bandeja, vibora, rulo (high balls), drop shot (low balls).

SECOND PART — TACTICAL POINTS (25 min)

Points 1: Net team uses all tools. Back team: glass win counts double.

Points 2: Tiebreak to 7. Net team must earn their position — only stay at net if they won the previous rally.

Last 10 min: King of the court.

modules.title: Module 5 — Mixed Review: Glass + Volleys + Serve (Beginner)
modules.description: Rotation drill across two courts combining back glass forehand and forehand volley. Serve review followed by King of the Court from the back.
modules.content:
MODULE 5 — MIXED REVIEW: GLASS + VOLLEYS + SERVE
Level: Beginner | Date: Tuesday 17 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Free rally, reduced court.

FIRST PART — ROTATION DRILL (25 min)

Court 1: Forehand off the back glass drill and rally (2 in the back, reduced court).

Court 2: Forehand volley drill and rally (1 at net, 1 in back, groundstrokes vs volleys).

Teams of 2 rotate between courts — 4 rotations total.

SECOND PART — SERVE + GAME (25 min)

Serve Practice: Same motion every time. 5 serves each, rotate.

Game: King of the court. Tiebreaks to 10. All start from the back. Coach feeds.

modules.title: Module 5 — Mixed Review: Glass + Volleys + Serve (Intermediate)
modules.description: Rotation drill combining side glass forehand and backhand with volley exchanges. Serve and return focus with glass bonus King of the Court.
modules.content:
MODULE 5 — MIXED REVIEW: GLASS + VOLLEYS + SERVE
Level: Intermediate | Date: Tuesday 17 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court rally going to the net (no overhead).

FIRST PART — ROTATION DRILL (25 min)

Court 1: Forehand and side glass drill and rally (2 in the back, aim down the line).

Court 2: Backhand and side glass drill and rally (1 in back, 1 at net, low shots vs volleys).

4 rotations.

SECOND PART — SERVE + GAME (25 min)

Serve and Return: One serves, partner returns to a target zone. Rotate every 5.

Game: King of the court. Glass win counts double. Tiebreaks to 10.

modules.title: Module 5 — Mixed Review: Glass + Volleys + Serve (Advanced)
modules.description: Focused rulo and drop shot decision-making drill for 1 and 2-player net combinations. Rule-based King of the Court with glass and rulo/drop shot scoring.
modules.content:
MODULE 5 — MIXED REVIEW: GLASS + VOLLEYS + SERVE
Level: Advanced | Date: Tuesday 17 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court with overheads.

FIRST PART — DROP SHOT + RULO FOCUSED (25 min)

Exercise 1: 1 at the net. High ball = rulo. Low ball = drop shot. Quick decisions. Rally: back varies, net uses the right shot every time.

Exercise 2: 2 at the net. Coach feeds lobs to random positions. Both communicate, decide who takes it and which shot. Rally: 2 in the back vs 2 at net — back varies, net team communicates.

SECOND PART — KING OF THE COURT (25 min)

Tiebreaks to 7.

Rule 1: Net team scores only if they used a rulo or drop shot in the rally.

Rule 2: Back team earns double points for winning off the glass.

modules.title: Module 6 — Transition: Defense to Attack (Beginner)
modules.description: First transition drills moving from glass defense to net attack. Tactical games rewarding the first team to reach and win at the net.
modules.content:
MODULE 6 — TRANSITION: DEFENSE TO ATTACK
Level: Beginner | Date: Friday 22 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Reduced court rally. Focus on footwork — recover position after every shot.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — TRANSITION INTRO
Drill: 1 in the back. Coach feeds deep. Player defends off glass and moves to net. Stop and reset each time.
Rally: 2 in the back. One defends off glass, both support and try to finish at the net.

EXERCISE 2 — SERVE
Focus on picking a direction. 5 serves each, rotate.

SECOND PART — TACTICAL GAME (25 min)

Tactical 1: 4 in the back. Coach feeds deep — return off glass, transition to net together.

Tactical 2: First team to transition to net and win the rally gets a bonus point.

If time left: Tiebreak to 10.

modules.title: Module 6 — Transition: Defense to Attack (Intermediate)
modules.description: Glass-to-net transition drills with lob progression. Tactical games with net win bonus points and lob-based defensive transitions.
modules.content:
MODULE 6 — TRANSITION: DEFENSE TO ATTACK
Level: Intermediate | Date: Friday 22 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court rally, going to the net included.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — TRANSITION
Drill: 1 in the back. Coach feeds deep — return off glass, move to net immediately.
Rally 1: 2 in the back. One defends off glass, both transition.
Rally 2: 2 in the back, 2 at net. Back starts with lob then transitions. Net lets lob bounce — defensive transition back.

EXERCISE 2 — SERVE + RETURN
Server picks direction. Returner plays to a target zone. Rotate every 5.

SECOND PART — TACTICAL GAME (25 min)

Tactical 1: 4 in the back. Return off glass, transition to net. Net win counts double.

Tactical 2: 2 in the back, 2 at net. Back team lob and transition. Net team defends and recovers. First to 5.

If time left: King of the court.

modules.title: Module 6 — Transition: Defense to Attack (Advanced)
modules.description: Net dominance drills with full shot selection communication under pressure. Tactical points where the net team must win from the net or lose a life.
modules.content:
MODULE 6 — TRANSITION: DEFENSE TO ATTACK
Level: Advanced | Date: Friday 22 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court with overheads.

FIRST PART — NET DOMINANCE (25 min)

Exercise 1: 2 at the net. Coach feeds random balls — high, low, middle. They communicate and play the right shot (volley, bandeja, vibora, rulo, or drop shot). Rally: 2 in back vs 2 at net. Net team attacks; back team defends using glass, lobs, variation.

Exercise 2 — Down the Line: 2 at net vs 1 in back. Defender plays two shots to the same player alternating direction. Forces direction control under pressure.

SECOND PART — FULL TACTICAL POINTS (25 min)

Points 1: Tiebreak to 7. Net team must win from the net — if pushed back, they lose a life.

Points 2: Tiebreak to 7. Free play — all shots. Focus on reading the game.

Last 10 min: King of the court.

modules.title: Module 7 — Mixed Game: All Skills Combined (Beginner)
modules.description: Random volley reaction drills and lob plus volley combination play. Points games with glass bonuses bringing together all learned skills.
modules.content:
MODULE 7 — MIXED GAME: ALL SKILLS COMBINED
Level: Beginner | Date: Tuesday 24 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Double touch rally in a reduced court.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — RANDOM VOLLEY
Drill: 1 at the net. Coach feeds randomly — forehand then backhand, no warning. Player reads and reacts.
Rally: 1 at net, 1 in back. Net mixes volleys; back aims low.

EXERCISE 2 — LOB + VOLLEY COMBO
Drill: 1 at net hitting lobs. Cone for recovery and target.
Rally: Back player: two low, one lob. Net player: two volleys, one bandeja.

SECOND PART — SERVE + POINTS (25 min)

Serve Practice: 3 serves each.

Game 1: 2 in back, 2 at net. Normal coach-fed points.

Game 2: Tiebreak to 10. All start from the back. Glass win counts double.

modules.title: Module 7 — Mixed Game: All Skills Combined (Intermediate)
modules.description: Glass plus volley drill and free variation rally with net player reading. Points games with double bonuses for glass and drop shots.
modules.content:
MODULE 7 — MIXED GAME: ALL SKILLS COMBINED
Level: Intermediate | Date: Tuesday 24 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court rally, including net approach.

FIRST PART — DRILL + RALLY (25 min)

EXERCISE 1 — GLASS + VOLLEY
Drill: 1 in back. Coach feeds to back glass. Player defends and aims deep.
Rally: 1 in back, 1 at net. Back uses glass when possible; net varies placement.

EXERCISE 2 — LOB + BANDEJA + VARIATION
Rally: 1 in back, 1 at net. Back varies freely (lob, glass, low groundstroke). Net reads and responds. Back cannot come to the net.

SECOND PART — SERVE + POINTS (25 min)

Serve and Return: Server aims to a zone. Returner picks a target. Rotate every 5.

Game 1: 2 in back, 2 at net. Glass win = double points. Drop shot win = double points.

Game 2: King of the court. Tiebreaks to 10.

modules.title: Module 7 — Mixed Game: All Skills Combined (Advanced)
modules.description: Full shot selection under pressure with rulo, drop shot, bandeja, and vibora. Tactical points with mandatory glass involvement from the back team.
modules.content:
MODULE 7 — MIXED GAME: ALL SKILLS COMBINED
Level: Advanced | Date: Tuesday 24 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court with overheads and smashes.

FIRST PART — RULO + DROP SHOT + TACTICAL (25 min)

Exercise 1: Coach feeds 3 balls — lob (rulo), low (drop shot), medium (bandeja or vibora). Player reads and executes quickly. Rally: 1 in back, 1 at net. Net uses all weapons freely.

Exercise 2: 2 at net vs 1 in back. Back player alternates direction every two shots. Net pair communicates and covers both sides.

SECOND PART — TACTICAL POINTS (25 min)

Points 1: Tiebreak to 7. Every rally must include at least one glass shot from the back team. If net wins without a glass shot — back team loses a bonus point.

Points 2: Free tiebreak to 7. Full tactical, no rules.

Last 10 min: King of the court.

modules.title: Module 8 — Match Play + King of the Court (Beginner)
modules.description: Final session combining a coach-selected drill review with open match play tiebreaks and King of the Court to close the program.
modules.content:
MODULE 8 — MATCH PLAY + KING OF THE COURT
Level: Beginner | Date: Friday 29 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Free rally, full court. Players choose their own warm-up.

FIRST PART — QUICK DRILL REVIEW (15 min)
Coach picks 2 drills based on group needs:
- Forehand or backhand off back glass
- Forehand or backhand volley
- Lob to lob rally

SECOND PART — MATCH PLAY (35 min)

Serve Practice: 3 serves each.

Match Play: Tiebreaks to 10. Coach feeds first ball. Rotate pairs every tiebreak.

King of the Court (last 15 min): Winners stay on. Challengers rotate in.

modules.title: Module 8 — Match Play + King of the Court (Intermediate)
modules.description: Final session with targeted drill review, match play tiebreaks with glass bonus, and King of the Court to 7.
modules.content:
MODULE 8 — MATCH PLAY + KING OF THE COURT
Level: Intermediate | Date: Friday 29 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court rally, full warm up.

FIRST PART — QUICK DRILL REVIEW (15 min)
Coach picks from:
- Forehand or backhand off glass and transition
- Volley sequence (random)
- Lob and bandeja rally

SECOND PART — MATCH PLAY (35 min)

Serve and Return: 3 serves each, focus on placement.

Match Play: Tiebreaks to 10. Glass win counts double.

King of the Court (last 15 min): Tiebreaks to 7. Winners stay on.

modules.title: Module 8 — Match Play + King of the Court (Advanced)
modules.description: Final session with tactical review of rulo, drop shot, and glass transition. Free tiebreak match play with optional scoring rules and King of the Court.
modules.content:
MODULE 8 — MATCH PLAY + KING OF THE COURT
Level: Advanced | Date: Friday 29 April 2026

WARM UP (10 min)
Full court with overheads and smashes.

FIRST PART — QUICK TACTICAL REVIEW (15 min)
Coach picks:
- Rulo under pressure
- Drop shot and recovery
- Transition from glass to net

SECOND PART — MATCH PLAY (35 min)

Points: Tiebreaks to 7. Free play — full tactical.

Optional final tiebreak rule: rulo or drop shot winner counts double.

King of the Court (last 15 min): Winners stay on.
